description: Structured index of major experiments for the corrector-timing thesis on ProSeCo-OWT. Entries are append-only; do not delete or rewrite past entries.

experiments.research_question: Measure per-step single-step correction value Delta_t and per-step pre-correction state signals along the base trajectory.
methods: Paired CRN single-correction branching, 50 seeds x T=64
experiments.interpretation: Establishes the per-step delta dataset and the basic per-step state features (entropy, inverse_margin, quality_mass_proxy, unmasked_fraction, n_revisable, n_masked). The base evidence used by every downstream audit.
supports: Existence of per-step Delta_t signal
Definition of trajectory state features
does_not_support: A deployable per-step trigger from these signals

experiments.research_question: Does the corrector schedule matter? Do separable signal rankers recover MC-oracle headroom over uniform?
methods: K=30 paired seeds; 10 greedy signal rankers; MC oracle = best of 100 random schedules

main_metrics.ranker_recovery: All separable rankers underperform; mean_delta_oracle saturates by B=8

experiments.interpretation: Timing matters: there is a substantial MC-oracle headroom over uniform. Separable rankers do not recover it. Establishes the Empirical Ranker-Class Limitation.
supports: Schedule headroom exists
Marginal rankers fail
does_not_support: Universal failure beyond the tested ranker class
experiments.decision_enabled: Phase 3a search baselines and Theorem A baseline.

experiments.research_question: Can a non-greedy search procedure with true-G feedback recover the MC-oracle headroom?
methods: CD-G (coordinate descent, true-G feedback); BS-AG (beam search, cheap-A pruning + true-G rollouts)
experiments.interpretation: Search using true-G feedback delivers most of the MC-oracle headroom. This is an existence result; CD-G/BS-AG are not deployable (use true G).
supports: Schedule-value function has exploitable structure that true-G search captures
does_not_support: A deployable scheduler from cheap features
experiments.decision_enabled: Interaction diagnostics to explain why rankers failed where search succeeded.

experiments.research_question: Are pair interactions xi_{s,t} = G({s,t}) - Delta_s - Delta_t non-zero and structured? Does a cheap pairwise surrogate Q(S) compose at schedule level?
targets: G(S) via pairwise Q at B in {2,3,4}
methods: 30 seeds x 73 stratified pairs (Gate 3a); no-leakage schedule-level Q validation (Gate 3b)
experiments.interpretation: Pair interactions are real and predominantly negative/redundant. Cheap low-d pairwise Q works at B=2 but fails at B=3,4 with the tested estimator family.
supports: Timing structure is partly interaction-driven
Redundancy is the dominant pair phenomenon
does_not_support: A direct cheap-pairwise scheduler at B>=3
Submodularity or BO-readiness

experiments.research_question: Does G(S) have a submodular/BO-ready structure or a clean local-neighborhood story?
targets: G(S) set-function diagnostics; one-swap neighborhood; DR triples
methods: Monotonicity comparisons; one-swap neighborhood; exact DR triples (Gate 5 K=10 HPC)
experiments.interpretation: Local structure exists but is weak; weak submodularity is not identifiable; BO is not justified as a thesis mainline.
supports: True-G search is the empirically successful family
Modest local smoothness
does_not_support: Weak-submodular guarantees
BO scheduler thesis claim

experiments.research_question: Do the 6 aggregate pre-correction state features improve held-out prediction of Delta_t beyond time alone?
methods: Grouped 5-fold by seed; cubic-time ridge baseline; state-augmented ridge; per-seed bootstrap CI on MSE reduction
experiments.interpretation: Under the tested linear class and current 6 aggregate state features, state does not add incremental signal to time for predicting Delta_t.
supports: No deployable single-step trigger from current 6 features under linear regression
does_not_support: State carries no timing info in general
Adaptive scheduling is impossible
experiments.decision_enabled: Pair-level state audit and enriched-feature audit.

experiments.research_question: Does pre-correction state at (s,t) predict pair interaction xi_{s,t} and G({s,t}) beyond pair geometry and A_pair?
methods: Grouped 5-fold seed CV; ridge baselines (geom, geom+A_pair, geom+A_pair+state); poly-2 ridge non-linear probe
experiments.interpretation: Geom + A_pair is a strong held-out predictor of xi (Spearman 0.69). The original 6 aggregate state features add nothing on top, and degree-2 polynomial expansion overfits with negative incremental signal.
supports: xi is predictable from (time geometry, A_pair) on held-out seeds
Aggregate pre-correction state adds nothing incremental at pair level under linear ridge
does_not_support: Richer feature classes are useless
Token-level features cannot help
experiments.decision_enabled: Enriched-feature audit covering derivatives, ratios, forward look, schedule-level.

experiments.backend: ProSeCo-OWT on A100 MIG (Bocconi gnode, SLURM job 493107)
experiments.research_question: Does the prepared extraction pipeline produce T0 aggregate features that match canonical Phase 1 trajectory_*.json values within abs-tolerance 1e-3 on K=2 seeds (RNG alignment gate)?
targets: alignment.rng_aligned == true on Phase 1 T0 fields
experiments.interpretation: All T0 aggregate features match canonical Phase 1 trajectory_*.json within 1e-6 (tolerance 1e-3). RNG is aligned on A100 CUDA. K=30 run authorised. Output archived as provenance during 2026-05-13 cleanup because the K=30 token-level run supersedes it.
supports: K=30 token-level targets (xi_raw.json) are reusable without joint rerun

experiments.backend: ProSeCo-OWT on A100 MIG (Bocconi gnode, SLURM job 493108)
experiments.research_question: Does token-level / model-internal pre-correction information (masked-position uncertainty shape, revisable-set uncertainty shape, concentration, pair-level Jaccard/cosine overlaps) improve held-out prediction of pair-level corrector-timing targets (xi and G_pair) beyond pair geometry + A_pair?
methods: extract_tokenlevel_features_proseco.py seeds 42-71 T=64 CUDA; grouped 5-fold seed CV; ablation P0..P6; ridge; per-seed bootstrap CI on MSE reduction
experiments.interpretation: Strong, clean negative. P1 (geometry + A_pair) achieves Spearman 0.693 on xi with MSE reduced 2.8 pp vs geometry alone. Every token-level family (T1-T4) reliably increases MSE relative to P1: CI [-0.0108, -0.0055] excludes zero, only 4/30 seeds improve. The negative effect is monotone across family additions. On G_pair the result is identical. Conclusion: correction timing on ProSeCo-OWT is captured by temporal geometry + additive marginal redundancy (A_pair). Model-internal token-level features (uncertainty shape, revisable-set, concentration, overlap) carry no incremental held-out signal under the tested ridge class.
supports: Pairwise timing interactions (xi) are strongly predicted by A_pair = Delta_s + Delta_t (Spearman 0.693)
Token-level model-internal features add no incremental held-out signal beyond A_pair on ProSeCo-OWT
does_not_support: Token-level features are useless on all backbones
Non-linear models cannot use this signal
Feature families beyond T0-T4 cannot help
experiments.decision_enabled: Stop feature search on ProSeCo-OWT. Four independent state-predictability negatives documented (Gate 6 marginal, pair, enriched, token-level). Write the ProSeCo-OWT diagnostic case study.
experiments.follow_up: Write / synthesise. ProSeCo-OWT case study: geometry + marginal-redundancy (A_pair) accounts for pairwise timing structure; search (CD-G/BS-AG) is the empirically successful scheduler family.

experiments.research_question: Does a richer family of locally derived pre-correction features (intensity ratios, causal derivatives, forward curvature, pair contrasts, schedule summaries) improve held-out prediction of corrector timing value at marginal, pair, and schedule levels beyond time geometry and marginal/additive baselines?
methods: Grouped 5-fold seed CV; ridge ablation across feature families S0/S1/S2/S3; curated poly-2 non-linear probe; per-seed bootstrap CI
experiments.interpretation: Enriched aggregate features (ratios, causal derivatives, forward differences, curvature, pair contrasts, schedule summaries) do not improve held-out prediction at any of the three target levels under the tested ridge / polynomial-ridge class. At B>=3 in the schedule audit, adding state on top of geometry + A(S) significantly HURTS held-out MSE. Sanity baselines (A_pair, A(S)) confirm the audit detects real signal where it exists. This is a triple-confirmed negative for aggregate observable state predictability of correction timing on ProSeCo-OWT.
supports: No incremental held-out signal from derived aggregate trajectory features beyond temporal geometry + marginal/additive baselines on this backbone, under the tested model class
Geometry + A_pair / A(S) are strong held-out predictors
does_not_support: Token-level / model-internal features cannot help
Non-aggregate features cannot help
Adaptive timing is impossible on other backbones
experiments.decision_enabled: Either (a) targeted token-level / logit-quantile inference pass on a tightly bounded HPC budget, or (b) writing the diagnostic case study with three independent state-predictability negatives.
experiments.follow_up: Next decision is supervisor-elective: token-level pilot vs. write.

experiments.backend: ProSeCo-OWT (local analysis of existing xi_raw.json, n=2190)
experiments.research_question: Does the pairwise interaction data reveal a saturation structure: do high-A_pair pairs show systematic redundancy (negative xi) while low-A_pair pairs retain genuine complementarity? Is G_pair a sublinear function of A_pair?
methods: OLS G_pair ~ A_pair; bootstrap CIs; quartile stratification; nested partial R2 (M0-M5); seed-level slope distribution; phase/distance stratification; baseline comparison
experiments.interpretation: Saturation structure is empirically confirmed on 4/5 gates. G_pair grows sublinearly in A_pair (slope=0.247, well below 1), Spearman(A_pair,xi)=-0.671. Q4 pairs are strongly redundant (mean xi=-0.277). Positive-A_pair Q1 pairs show genuine complementarity (P(G>0)=0.814). 86.7% of seeds show positive G_vs_A slope. C2 is borderline: R2=0.0925 vs threshold 0.10; this is due to phase being a large confounder (adding phase boosts R2 to 0.150). The saturation story is solid. Phase B (corrector-strength experiment) is the natural next step but requires supervisor sign-off given the borderline C2.
supports: Sublinear G_pair composition = saturation signature
High-A_pair redundancy is real and strong
Low-A_pair complementarity is genuine when A_pair>0
Saturation is consistent across seeds and phase/distance strata
does_not_support: A deployable pairwise scheduler from A_pair alone
Linear R2 is a sufficient saturation diagnostic (phase confound masks it)
experiments.decision_enabled: Phase B (corrector-strength experiment) pending C2 threshold discussion with supervisor.
experiments.follow_up: Discuss C2 borderline with supervisor. If proceeding: implement Phase B corrector-steps={0,1,2,3} experiment.

experiments.research_question: Do corrector strength variants (corrector_steps in {0,1,2}) produce meaningfully different correction behaviours, and does strength_1 reproduce canonical Protocol A values?
methods: TrackedGenerator wrapper tracks per-call token changes without consuming RNG
CRN: predictor steps unchanged across strength variants (corrector is deterministic)
Seeds 42,43; T=64; 3 pairs per A_pair stratum (low/mid/high); all 4 strength levels

preflight_gates.G3_strength_variants_differ: PASS (s0=4.07, s1=32.57, s2=46.03 mean changed tokens)
preflight_gates.G5_crn_consistent: PASS (f_base identical across all strengths per seed)

experiments.interpretation: All 5 preflight gates pass. Strength variants produce meaningfully different behaviours: corrector_n_changed jumps 8x from s0 (4.07) to s1 (32.57), then 1.4x from s1 to s2 (46.03). Stronger correction increases G_pair but xi becomes more negative (-0.025 -> -0.091 -> -0.130), confirming that stronger correction amplifies additive marginal effects faster than interaction gains. Output archived as provenance during 2026-05-13 cleanup because the K=30 corrector-strength run supersedes it. CRN is perfectly preserved (G5 pass, G1 max_error=0.0).
supports: Strength variants are scientifically non-trivial and distinguishable
CRN preservation confirmed: corrector is deterministic, f_base identical across strengths
Saturation signature (negative ξ) deepens monotonically with corrector strength
Canonical Protocol A Δ_t values reproduced exactly by strength_1
does_not_support: Stronger correction reduces interaction redundancy
strength_0 shows complementarity (P(ξ>0)=0.0 in preflight)
experiments.decision_enabled: Main Phase B K=30 experiment authorised (all 5 preflight gates pass).
experiments.follow_up: Main Phase B K=30 experiment: job 493234. See corrector_strength_k30 entry.

experiments.research_question: Does corrector strength (corrector_steps ∈ {0,1,2}) modulate the pairwise interaction structure (ξ, G_pair, A_pair) in a scientifically interpretable way across 30 seeds?
targets: G_pair, A_pair, xi per (seed, pair_id, strength)
methods: CorrectorStrengthGenerator: TrackedGenerator subclass, corrector is deterministic → CRN preserved
30 seeds (42-71), T=64, 9 pairs per seed (3 per A_pair stratum), 4 strength levels
Single-shot model load, corrector_steps mutated between strengths

preflight_gates.G3_strength_variants_differ: PASS (s0=3.02, s1=30.82, s2=42.46 mean changed tokens)

experiments.interpretation: Corrector strength modulates G_pair and A_pair proportionally but does NOT change mean ξ between s1 and s2 (−0.0289 vs −0.0290, essentially identical). The s0→s1 jump is 10× in corrector_n_changed (3.02→30.82) — the annealed refinement step is where the bulk of token-changing work occurs. P(ξ>0) grows monotonically: 0.126 (s0) → 0.307 (s1) → 0.367 (s2), showing more correction reveals more complementarity. But mean ξ saturates: going from s1 to s2 does not shift the average interaction structure, only rescales amplitudes uniformly. The saturation hypothesis is confirmed: the corrector operates within a fixed interaction geometry; scaling its strength neither resolves nor deepens cross-timestep redundancy beyond the s1 operating point.
supports: Corrector strength increases G_pair and A_pair in proportion (ξ unchanged at s1→s2)
P(ξ>0) grows with strength: more correction reveals more complementarity
Corrector efficacy is non-linear: 10× jump s0→s1 in corrector_n_changed, only 1.4× s1→s2
CRN perfectly preserved across all strength levels (G1 max_error=0.0, G5 pass)
No_correction is a perfect no-op (G4 pass, max_abs_delta=0.0)
does_not_support: Stronger correction resolves pairwise redundancy (mean ξ unchanged s1→s2)
Corrector strength is a lever for improving interaction structure beyond s1
experiments.decision_enabled: Phase B conclusion: corrector strength does not provide a new lever for improving pairwise complementarity beyond the canonical s1 setting. The interaction geometry is fixed; scheduling strategy (when to apply the corrector) remains the key variable.
experiments.follow_up: Synthesise Phase A + Phase B into thesis narrative. Write ProSeCo-OWT diagnostic case study: saturation structure is fixed by trajectory geometry (A_pair), not by corrector intensity.
